title: Time & Work
description: Calculate task durations, worker rates, and joint effort efficiencies.
theory: Work Done = Rate (Efficiency) × Time. Efficiency is inversely proportional to time taken.

shortcuts.title: The LCM Method
shortcuts.formula: Total Work = LCM of individual times.
shortcuts.explanation: Saves time by converting fractions into whole numbers.

example.problem: A takes 10 days, B takes 15. Together?
example.stepByStep: LCM=30. A's rate=3, B's=2. Combined=5. Time = 30/5 = 6 days.

title: Pipes & Cisterns
description: Calculate tank filling rates, leaking speeds, and tap coordination.
theory: Inlets perform positive work (+ rate); outlets/leaks perform negative work (- rate).

shortcuts.title: Net Filling Rate
shortcuts.explanation: Use LCM to compute net volume gain per hour.

example.problem: Inlet 8 hrs, Outlet 12 hrs. Net time?
example.stepByStep: LCM=24. Inlet=+3, Outlet=-2. Net=+1 unit/hr. Time = 24 / 1 = 24 hours.

title: Percentages
description: Solve percentage comparison scales and growth/decline fractions.
theory: Percentage = parts per 100. Percentage change = (Change / Original) × 100.

shortcuts.title: Percentage Less comparison
shortcuts.explanation: Calculates how much less B is than A when A is R% more than B.

example.problem: A's salary is 25% more than B's. B's is how much less?

title: Profit & Loss
description: Calculate margins, markups, successive discount percentages, and cost prices.
theory: Profit% = (Profit / CP) × 100. SP = CP × (1 + Profit%). SP = MP × (1 - Discount%).

shortcuts.title: Net profit AB Formula
shortcuts.explanation: Determines final margin when markup and discount percentages are given.

example.problem: Markup 30%, discount 10%. Profit?

title: Boats & Streams
description: Determine upstream and downstream speeds and current velocities.
theory: Let boat = U, stream = V. Downstream (D) = U + V. Upstream (Up) = U - V.

shortcuts.title: Finding Boat & Current Rates
shortcuts.explanation: Derives still-water speeds directly from trip speeds.

example.problem: Downstream = 15 km/h, Upstream = 9 km/h. Find Boat U.

title: Simple & Compound Interest
description: Calculate simple yields, compounded interest, and yield differences.
theory: Simple Interest (SI) = P×R×T/100. Compound Interest (CI) Amount = P(1 + R/100)^T.

shortcuts.title: 2-Year CI/SI Difference
shortcuts.explanation: Computes 2-year compounding margins directly.

example.problem: Difference on $4000 at 5% for 2 years?

title: Mixtures & Allegations
description: Solve blending ratios of commodities with different pricing.
theory: Calculate ratios to mix a cheaper commodity and a dearer commodity to achieve a target mean price.

shortcuts.title: Allegation Formula
shortcuts.explanation: Subtract along diagonals in allegation cross grids.

example.problem: Mix $50/kg and $80/kg tea to get a $62/kg blend.

title: Probability
description: Evaluate chances of independent and conditional events.
theory: P(Event) = Favorable outcomes / Total outcomes. Value lies between 0 (impossible) and 1 (certain).

shortcuts.title: Independent events multiplication
shortcuts.explanation: Multiply probabilities for consecutive independent trials.

example.problem: P of getting 2 heads in 2 flips?

title: Permutations & Combinations
description: Arrange (permutations) and select (combinations) groups of items.
theory: Permutations (Order matters): nPr = n!/(n-r)!. Combinations (Order doesn't matter): nCr = n!/(r! × (n-r)!).

shortcuts.title: Quick Selection
shortcuts.explanation: Shortcut for choosing exactly 2 items from n choices.

example.problem: Ways to choose 2 leaders from 6?

title: Partnerships
description: Distribute company profits based on capital investment and timeline.
theory: Profit ratio = (Capital 1 × Time 1) : (Capital 2 × Time 2).

shortcuts.title: Simplified Capital Ratio
shortcuts.explanation: Use if investments are held for equal time durations.

example.problem: A invests $3000, B invests $6000. Profit share ratio?

title: HCF & LCM
description: Solve HCF/LCM fractions, divisibility indices, and factors.
theory: HCF = Highest Common Factor (largest divisor). LCM = Lowest Common Multiple (smallest multiple).

shortcuts.title: Product Relation
shortcuts.explanation: Directly relates HCF/LCM of two numbers to their product.

example.problem: HCF is 4, LCM is 24, one number is 8. Find the other.

title: Speed, Time & Distance
description: Calculate speed conversions, relative speeds, and travel times.
theory: Distance = Speed × Time. km/h to m/s = Multiply by 5/18.

shortcuts.title: Average Speed formula
shortcuts.explanation: Average speed for round trips of equal distance at speeds X and Y.

example.problem: Go at 30 km/h, return at 60 km/h. Avg speed?

title: Ages
description: Solve age shifts and ratio equations across timelines.
theory: Setup linear equations where age differences remain constant over time.

shortcuts.title: Constant difference method
shortcuts.explanation: The age gap between two individuals never changes over time.

example.problem: Father is 30, son is 5. When will father be twice son's age?
example.stepByStep: Difference = 25. Twice means son is 25, so in 20 years.

title: Averages
description: Deduce average values, mixtures, and replaced elements.
theory: Average = Sum of values / Count. Sum = Average × Count.

shortcuts.title: Replacement Shortcut
shortcuts.explanation: Solves replacement questions instantly without sums.

example.problem: Average of 10 people goes +1.5kg when replacing a 50kg person.

title: Simplification & Approximation
description: Evaluate complex calculations using priority orders and estimates.
theory: Follow BODMAS (Brackets, Orders, Division, Multiplication, Addition, Subtraction) priorities.

shortcuts.title: BODMAS order
shortcuts.explanation: Always respect mathematical order of operations.

title: Coding-Decoding
description: Decipher letter shifts, alphanumeric representations, and word codes.
theory: Analyze letter displacements (e.g. +2 shifts or reversals) to decode patterns.

shortcuts.title: EJOTY Landmark
shortcuts.explanation: Memorize EJOTY positions to reference characters quickly.

example.problem: Find position of T.

title: Syllogisms
description: Test deductions and statement overlaps using Venn diagrams.
theory: Examine if conclusions must hold true based on categorical premises.

shortcuts.title: Minimal Overlap
shortcuts.explanation: A conclusion must be valid in ALL valid diagram configurations.

example.problem: All A are B. Some B are C. Does 'Some A are C' follow?
example.stepByStep: No, A and C can be drawn with zero overlap. Thus, it does not follow.

title: Clocks
description: Calculate hour-minute hand angles and angular speeds.
theory: Minute hand speed = 6°/min. Hour hand speed = 0.5°/min. Hour hand covers 30°/hour.

shortcuts.title: Angle Formula
shortcuts.explanation: Instantly computes angular separation between hands at H:M.

example.problem: Angle at 2:20?

title: Calendars
description: Identify week days, leap years, and calendar cycles.
theory: Non-leap year = 1 odd day. Leap year = 2 odd days. Cycles repeat every 7 days.

shortcuts.title: Odd Days Weekday Finder
shortcuts.explanation: Discard full weeks by finding remainder of days divided by 7.

example.problem: If today is Friday, what day is after 30 days?

title: Data Sufficiency
description: Evaluate if provided statements contain sufficient data to solve a problem.
theory: Verify if Statement 1 alone, Statement 2 alone, both, or neither provide enough data.

shortcuts.title: Data Check Map
shortcuts.explanation: Analyze statements independently before combining them.

example.problem: Find X. (1) X is even. (2) X is a prime number between 1 and 5.
example.stepByStep: (1) alone: infinite. (2) alone: 2 or 3. Combined: X must be 2. Sufficient.

title: Number Series
description: Decipher patterns in progressions and sequences.
theory: Look for differences, multiplier ratios, squares/cubes, or alternating terms.

shortcuts.title: Difference Check
shortcuts.explanation: Start by writing differences to check for simple additions.

title: Direction Sense
description: Track coordinate movements and displacements.
theory: Trace movements on a N-E-S-W grid. Use Pythagorean calculations for diagonal paths.

shortcuts.title: Pythagorean displacement
shortcuts.explanation: Calculate straight line displacement using right triangles.

example.problem: Walk 3m North, then 4m East. Distance?

title: Seating Arrangements
description: Solve row positionings and circular seating arrangements.
theory: Map seating grids starting with absolute references (ends/centers) before placing relatives.

shortcuts.title: Circular directions
shortcuts.formula: Facing center: Clockwise = Left; Counter-Clockwise = Right
shortcuts.explanation: Orientation directions for inward-facing circle games.

example.problem: A sits to the immediate left of B facing center.
example.stepByStep: Draw B on circle. Place A clockwise next to B.

title: Blood Relations
description: Draw family trees and decode relational statements.
theory: Build family trees using gender nodes (+ for Male, - for Female) and generation levels.

shortcuts.title: Backward Decryption
shortcuts.explanation: Trace descriptors backwards starting with 'my ...' to simplify relations.

example.problem: Daughter of my father's sister?
example.stepByStep: father's sister = Aunt. Aunt's daughter = Cousin.

title: Subject-Verb Agreement
description: Match subjects and verbs in singular or plural counts.
theory: Singular subjects require singular verbs; plural subjects require plural verbs.

shortcuts.title: The Bracket Test
shortcuts.formula: Cross out parenthetical clauses in commas.
shortcuts.explanation: Ignore details like 'along with', 'of the' to isolate true subject.

example.problem: The cat, along with the kittens, (is/are) sleeping.
example.stepByStep: Cross out 'along with the kittens'. 'The cat' is singular → use 'is'.

title: Spotting Errors
description: Identify prepositional, grammatical, and word choice errors.
theory: Locate incorrect parts of sentences by examining modifier placements, tenses, and pronoun cases.

shortcuts.title: Between vs. Among
shortcuts.explanation: Use 'between' for dual pairs and 'among' for groups of three or more.

example.problem: Distribute books (between/among) 3 children.

title: Synonyms & Antonyms
description: Build synonyms and antonym associations using root word contexts.
theory: Synonyms are words with similar meanings. Antonyms are words with opposite meanings.

shortcuts.title: Prefix Analysis
shortcuts.explanation: Use root semantics to deduce meanings contextually.

example.problem: Meaning of Malevolent?
example.stepByStep: Prefix 'mal' means bad. The word means wishing evil or harm to others.
